title: Sternberg Working Memory
description: This dataset contains scalp EEG recordings from 23 healthy young adults performing a modified Sternberg working memory task. Participants selectively memorized sequences of black letters while ignoring green distractor letters, then responded to probe letters during a recognition phase. The study investigates source-resolved EEG brain dynamics associated with working memory encoding, maintenance, and retrieval processes, with data collected at multiple sampling rates (250-1000 Hz) from 71 electrode channels.
methods_description: Scalp EEG data were collected from 71 channels (69 scalp and 2 periocular electrodes) referred to right mastoid at sampling rates of 250 Hz (subjects 1-12), 1000 Hz (subject 14), or 500 Hz (subjects 15-24), with analog passband 0.01-100 Hz. Participants performed a modified Sternberg task consisting of 3-4 blocks of 25 trials each, with each trial including letter sequence presentation (8 letters over 1.2s each with 0.2s ISI), memory maintenance (2-4s), probe presentation, and auditory feedback.
